articles.title: 'Ghostcommit' hides prompt injection in images to fool AI agents, steal secrets
articles.summary: A PNG hiding a prompt injection could steal your repo's secrets, researchers demonstrate. The technique, dubbed 'Ghostcommit,' slipped past AI code reviewers CodeRabbit and Bugbot, which never open image files at all, then convinced a coding agent to read a repo's .env and write every secret into the code as a list of numbers. articles.title: GHSA: melange: Incomplete package integrity verification allows data section substitution

An attacker who could compromise a mirror, poison a cache, or MITM a package fetch could substitute arbitrary file contents while the control hash check still passed.

articles.title: GHSA: mkfifo: permissions of an existing file are changed after FIFO creation fails
articles.summary:

```
$ touch secret; chmod 000 secret
$ coreutils mkfifo secret fifo3 fifo4
mkfifo: cannot create fifo 'secret': File exists
$ ll secret
